seasonality: Waterfront weather can be windy, so keep layers and indoor food stops available.

transport_advice: Keep waterfront stops grouped to avoid repeated hill climbs and cross-city transfers.

planning_tips: A waterfront day should balance scenic stops with food breaks rather than stacking too many distant neighborhoods.

title: San Francisco Waterfront and Fisherman's Wharf Planning Notes
content: San Francisco Travel highlights visitor planning around attractions, weather, safety, public transportation, Fisherman's Wharf, Coit Tower, Golden Gate experiences, and waterfront activities. For itinerary planning, waterfront stops should be grouped to preserve pacing and reduce unnecessary transfers.

seasonality: Fog and wind can make layered clothing useful even in warmer months.

transport_advice: Golden Gate Park attractions are best grouped together rather than split across multiple distant neighborhoods.

planning_tips: For a relaxed San Francisco day, combine one major museum with one or two park stops instead of trying to cover the entire city.

title: San Francisco Golden Gate Park and Museum Planning Notes
content: San Francisco Travel describes attractions such as world-class museums, Fisherman's Wharf, Golden Gate Park, botanical gardens, and art museums. Golden Gate Park is a strong same-day cluster for museum, outdoor, and relaxed-pace itineraries because it combines indoor and outdoor options in one area.
